seo_description: Current time in Omaha, Nebraska. Central Time Zone — CST (UTC-6) in winter, CDT (UTC-5) during DST. Live clock and time differences for Omaha, NE.

faq.question: What time is it in Omaha right now?
faq.answer: Omaha, Nebraska, USA uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) in winter and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Omaha is on the same time as Chicago, Dallas, and Kansas City — 1 hour behind New York and 1 hour ahead of Denver. The live clock above shows the current Omaha time.

faq.question: What time zone is Omaha in?
faq.answer: Omaha, Nebraska is in the Central Time Zone (CT). It uses Central Standard Time (CST, UTC-6) from the first Sunday in November to the second Sunday in March, and Central Daylight Time (CDT, UTC-5) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Chicago. Omaha shares its time zone with Chicago, Kansas City, and Minneapolis.

faq.question: Does Omaha observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Omaha, Nebraska observes Daylight Saving Time. Clocks spring forward 1 hour on the second Sunday in March at 2:00 AM (CST → CDT) and fall back on the first Sunday in November. Nebraska follows the standard US DST schedule.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Omaha and New York?
faq.answer: Omaha (CST/CDT, Central Time) is always 1 hour behind New York (EST/EDT, Eastern Time). Both cities observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the gap constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in New York, it is 8:00 AM in Omaha.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Omaha and Denver?
faq.answer: Omaha (CST/CDT, Central Time) is always 1 hour ahead of Denver (MST/MDT, Mountain Time). Both cities observe DST on the same schedule, keeping the gap constant year-round. When it is 9:00 AM in Denver, it is 10:00 AM in Omaha.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Omaha?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Omaha are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM CT, Monday through Friday. Omaha is Nebraska's largest city and a major financial and insurance hub. Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway is headquartered in Omaha, along with Mutual of Omaha, First National Bank of Omaha, and TD Ameritrade (now Schwab). The ConAgra Foods and Union Pacific Railroad headquarters are also in Omaha, reflecting its strong agriculture and logistics industries.
